title: Selective Isolation of Lipid Classes from cold-water corals via SPE for Quantitative Fatty Acid Profiling
author: Audrey M. Pruski

steps_raw.instruction: Sample Preparation
Flash-freeze Sample on board to prevent degradation
Freeze-dry samples and store at -80 °C until analysis
Grind freeze-dried Sample into a fine powder using a bead mill homogenizer while keeping the chamber filled with liquid nitrogen to prevent heating
Preparation of Working Solutions
Chloroform/methanol/water (1:2:0.8) reagent: 5.265 mL CHCl₃, 10.525 mL CH 3 OH and 4.21 mL H₂O. Keep at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator
1M NaCl solution in ultrapure water (58g.L -1 ). Keep at 4 °C in a refrigerator
0.3N NaOH solution (12 g.L-1). Keep at 4 °C in a refrigerator
0.2M H₃PO₄ solution (19.6g.L-1). Keep at 4 °C in a refrigerator
BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene) at 50 mg.mL-1 in Chloroform. Keep at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator
Total lipid extraction Total lipids are extracted using a modified version of the Bligh and Dyer method (1959), optimized to improve extraction efficiency.
Place the freeze-dried Sample (~ 3 g ) in a 30 mL glass tube (round bottom, screw tap with PTFE liner)
Add 20 mL of CHCl₃/CH₃OH/H 2 O (1:2:0.8) with 1M NaCl + 0.2M H₃PO₄ The solution of NaCl 1M-H 3 PO 4 0.2M increases protein unwinding and lipid recovery
Sonicate 00:05:00 ( On ice, if needed ), then rotate 00:20:00 at Room temperature
Add 2.5 mL CHCl₃, then 2.5 mL H₂O. Shake 00:05:00
2500 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Recover the organic phase (bottom) using a Pasteur pipette
Repeat extraction 3 times with 5 mL CHCl₃
Pool all organic phases (final volume ≈ 22.765 mL) and vortex. ⚠ Keep organic phase cold On ice
Wash organic phase with 2.5 mL cold 0.3N NaOH. Shake 00:01:00
2500 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes and recover the organic phase
Take 20 mL or more into a new glass tube (conical bottom, screw tap with PTFE liner) and evaporate at 40 °C under vacuum (rotary evaporator) ⚠ Lipid extracts should never be allowed to dry out!
Cool at -20 °C and redissolve in 400 µL CHCl₃ + BHT (50 mg.L -1 ) BHT is added optionally to chloroform to prevent lipid oxidation during extraction!
Centrifuge 2000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes Store at -20 °C
Solvent Preparation

Solvent A: Chloroform:2-propanol (2:1 v/v) Solvent B: 2% Acetic acid in diethyl ether Solvent C: Methanol Solvent D: Hexane Solvent E: 1% Diethyl ether + 10% Dichloromethane in hexane Solvent F: 5% Ethyl acetate in hexane Solvent G: 15% Ethyl acetate in hexane Solvent H: Chloroform:Methanol (2:1 v/v) Keep all solvents at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator!

Separation of neutral lipids, fatty acids and phospholipids 3 Strata NH₂ (aminopropyl silica) cartridges (Phenomenex) are needed per sample. 7 lipid fractions are efficiently recovered from the initial chloroform extract with minimal impurities.
Condition the first cartridge (C1) with 2 × 2 mL hexane, vacuum dry 2–5 min
Load 390 µL lipid extract. Vacuum to dryness
Elute neutral lipids (E1): 4 × 2 mL solvent A
Elute free fatty acids (E2): 4 × 2 mL solvent B
Elute phospholipids (E3): 4 × 2 mL solvent C
Store eluates at -20 °C .

Neutral Lipid Fractionation In the original method by Kaluzny et al. (1985), the elution of steryl esters was demonstrated using commercially available lipid standards, which did not include wax esters. However, based on our experimental results, wax esters appear to co-elute with steryl esters under the same solvent conditions used for fraction E4.
Condition a second cartridge (C2) as above with hexane
Load 390 µL from E1. No need to dry
Elute wax esters and sterol esters (E4): 4 × 2 mL solvent D Note: Mono-, di-, and triglycerides and sterols are retained on C2.
Condition a third cartridge (C3) as above
Stack C2 onto C3 using an adapter
Elute triglycerides (E5): 6 × 2 mL solvent E
Elute sterols (E6): 9 × 2 mL solvent F
Remove and discard C3, place C2 back on manifold
Elute diglycerides (E7): 4 × 2 mL solvent G
Elute monoglycerides (E8): 4 × 2 mL solvent H
Evaporation of lipid fractions
Evaporate under vacuum all eluates ( 45 °C )

Redissolve in 100 µL CHCl₃ + BHT. Store at -20 °C Eluates for GC-MS analysis of fatty acids are E2 (Free Fatty Acids), E3 (Phospholipids), E4 (Wax esters and Sterol esters), E5 (Triglycerides), E7 (Diglycerides), and E8 (Monoglycerides). E6 may be used for sterol analysis!

Preparation of Working Solutions - Transmethylation reagent: 3.4 mL Methanol + 0.6 mL H₂SO₄ (98%), and 4.0 mL CHCl₃ with BHT (50 mg/L). Keep at 4 °C in a flammable storage refrigerator Reagent should be cold and freshly prepared - C19:0 (internal standard) stock solution ~1 mg.mL ‑1 in hexane. The solution is diluted before use (1/9). Keep at -20 °C Adjust the concentration of the internal standard so that its peak on the chromatogram falls within the range of peak intensities, ideally between the most abundant and the least abundant fatty acids. This ensures accurate quantification across the entire dynamic range
To the eluates: add 20 µL internal standard C19:0 + 8 mL of transmethylation reagent. Cap and shake
Incubate 01:30:00 at 90 °C . Shake occasionally (3-4 times)
Cool ( On ice or -20 °C ). Open carefully
Recover lower organic phase in a new glass tube
Organic Phase Wash
Lipid extraction and Transesterification
Phase Separation
Add 2 mL cold H₂O. Vortex. 300 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes Centrifuge 5 min, 3000 rpm, 4°C

Fatty acid methyl extracts are ready for GC-MS analysis In this study, the analyses were carried out using the instrumental conditions described in a companion protocol developed by our group (Pruski & Vétion, 2024).
Transfer to GC vials with a 200 µl insert. Store at -80 °C
Redissolve in 30-50 µL pure hexane. Vortex, 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Evaporate solvent in a SpeedVac concentrator at Room temperature . Cool at -20 °C
Discard the upper polar layer and transfer 6-9 mL of the lower organic phase to a clean screw-cap tube
Wash with 4 mL cold 2% K₂CO₃. 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Combine all organic phases (~8–9 mL)
Re-extract aqueous phase 2 times with 2 mL cold hexane:CHCl₃ (4:1)
Final Purification
Drying and Dissolving FAMEs
